Heavy Flamer: More akin the flamers used BattleMechs than the ma pack; the heawy flamer covers the same area but Inflicts considerably more damage_ The fuel used even more volatile , making nazardous to its crew: If the crew is hit Dy ranged attack; the gamemaster rolls 2010_ result of 18 greater; the heavy flamer's fuel source explodes and causes the heavy flamer" damage blast effect (damage type X} from the crew as the point of impact: Recoil Compensators Burst-fire weapons are effective, but the recoil generated finng stream of projectiles at high speed massive. Many devices have been developed t0 reduce the effect of recoil; with varying degrees of success_ One of the most common kinds of recoil compensation gas-vent system that vents the weapon barrel gases at specific vector t0 counter barrel climb_ Support weapons can benefit from gyroscopic harness mounts_ In game terms, if wcapon equipped with any recoil com pensation, it increases the cost ad weight of the weapon by 10 percent; but the weapon' recoil rating increased Dy one point. weapon with recoil rating of 2 (+1 TN Modifier for every two shots fired burst) titted with recoil compensation would have recoil rating of 3 and suffer instead +1 penalty per three shots tired. In addition recoil compensation makes weapon bulkier and more difficult to conceal: apply -3 TN Moditier t0 tne Perception Check any character trying spot such concealed weapon; Sonic Stunner The sonic stunner; like the stunstick; i5 nonlethal weapon meant render target unconscious It projects an ultra high frequency sound that can stun 
[PDF_PAGE 54 | score=8] CHARACTER CREATION OCCUPATIONAL FIELDS List Occupational fields consist of several skills that are most commonly used when per forming particular job_ The following lists do not include every conceivable skill related t0 that field but rather the skills most strongly associated with field that are used most often in day t0 day operations When character receives field during the Life Path, he or she gets three Skill Points for every skill associated with that field. The following list is sample of the most common occupational fields available_ Gamemasters should feel free t0 create new ones as needed t0 represent occupations appropriate t0 their campaigns: Prerequisites are provided for some fields for reference purposes. They help explain why certain skill packages ack what might seem to be basic skills: for example the Infantry Field lacks the basic skill Rifles because characters have already received that skill in the pre required Basic Training Field_ Occupational fields are divided into military and civilian groups alphabetized within each group for ease of reference- MILITARY FIELDs Basic Training Career / Soldier First Aid Navigation /Ground Rifles Martial Arts/Miitary Infantry Prerequisite: Basic Training AnMeny Climbing Comms Conv: Uump Packs Support Weapons Tactics/ Infantry Aerospace Fighter Pilot Prerequisite: Basic Training Gunnery / Ballistic , Heno Gunnery / Laser / Aeio Gunnery / Missile / Aero Navigation/Air Navigation / Space Piloting /Aero Sensor Operations Tactics Space Zero G Operations Cavalry Prerequisite; Basic Training Artillery Gunnery /Ballistic /Conv: Gunnery/Laser/ Conv Gunnery /Missile /Conv: Piloting / choose Sensor Operations Technician/Mechanics Hover Naval, Sub.
